decomposition.intent: Introduce the runner-owned input-source resolution seam that turns a (source, raw|transformed) reference into a frame plus a provenance ref, with materialize-if-absent and a transformed-output result_id tied into the RunResult scheme.
decomposition.scope: New resolve_frame(source, raw|transformed) -> (frame, ref). raw resolves to the instance table; transformed resolves to the latest staging table, else runs the source's pipeline once to materialize it (cycle-guarded, snapshot semantics). ref carries a deterministic UUID5 result_id derived with the RunResult identity helper. Does NOT change the join yet.
acceptance: resolve_frame(source, raw) returns the source's instance table contents.
resolve_frame(source, transformed) returns the source's latest staging table contents when one exists.
resolve_frame(source, transformed) for a source with no staging table runs that source's pipeline once and returns its produced output (materialize-if-absent).
A transformed reference forming a cycle (A->C->A) raises an error naming the sources in the cycle and does not loop.
The ref returned for a transformed frame carries a deterministic UUID5 result_id (equal inputs -> equal id) consistent with the RunResult identity scheme.
resolve_frame returns correct rows over null-containing / type-messy real-world data without dropping or corrupting rows.

decomposition.intent: Fix the dead use_transformed join toggle by reading the right-hand source through resolve_frame, and make the right-column-fetch endpoint return transformed columns when the flag is set.
decomposition.scope: _execute_join and _validate_join_config resolve the right source via resolve_frame per use_transformed (raw -> instance table; transformed -> resolved frame). The column-fetch endpoint the join modal already calls with the transformed flag returns the transformed column set. Frontend is regression-only.
acceptance: A Join with use_transformed=false joins against the right source's raw instance table (behavior unchanged).
A Join with use_transformed=true joins against the right source's transformed frame resolved via resolve_frame.
A transformed Join against a never-run right source materializes it on demand and the join output reflects the right source's transforms.
The right-column-fetch endpoint returns the right source's transformed column set when the transformed flag is set, and the raw columns when not.
JoinModal still sends use_transformed and requests the transformed column set (frontend regression).
Join over null-containing / type-messy join keys behaves correctly (messy real-world data).

decomposition.intent: Replace the inline if/elif step dispatch in run_pipeline with a uniform StepExecutor contract resolved from a step-type registry, backed by class-based Step/StepContext with factory constructors over the existing map tables.
decomposition.scope: StepContext.from_builtin (source_builtin_map) / from_function / from_set (source_function_map); a StepExecutor per step type registered in a step-type registry; run_pipeline dispatches through the registry. Behavior-preserving for existing function and built-in steps.
acceptance: Function and built-in steps dispatched through the StepExecutor registry produce per-result content identical to pre-refactor (each entry's function_type, status, row counts, identity), with transforms and validations preserved as distinct RunResult/ValidationRunResult types; per-member emission ORDER may interleave (a mixed set is no longer grouped transforms-then-validations — it behaves as if its members were placed individually, per slice 4).

StepContext.from_builtin constructs a context from a source_builtin_map row; from_function / from_set construct from source_function_map rows, carrying the properties previously passed as step-dict keys.

The inline if/elif step dispatch is removed from run_pipeline; each result entry's content is unchanged on a mixed function+builtin pipeline (per-member ordering may interleave; the transform/validation split into distinct result types is preserved).
Each superseded dispatch path removed in this slice is covered by a behavior-preserving test asserting equal output before and after.
decomposition.reconciliation_note: 2026-06-16 (post-to-code, hostile-audit kickback): AC#0/#2 originally said 'results identical to pre-refactor output', which implied step ORDER preservation. The refactor interleaves results by member (slice-4 'as if individual'), so a mixed set's emission order changed from grouped transforms-then-validations. User decision: interleaving is intended; the real invariant is per-result content identical + transform/validation split preserved (resolved via RunResult vs ValidationRunResult, not a new object). AC reworded to match; an order-blindness-is-intentional marker added to the golden test. Conflict between slice-3 (preserve) and slice-4 (individual) resolved in favor of slice 4.

decomposition.intent: Flatten a function set into a stream of uniform member executions through the StepExecutor contract, so a set behaves exactly like its members placed individually, with the member dispatch type-agnostic so built-in members are additive later.
decomposition.scope: An adapter that expands a set into per-member StepContexts dispatched through the registry by each member's step type. Heterogeneous-member readiness only; storing a built-in inside a set (schema/persistence) is out of scope (#275).
acceptance: A function set is flattened into per-member executions whose results are identical to the same functions placed individually on the source.
run_pipeline output for a set-containing pipeline is unchanged from pre-refactor.

The adapter dispatches each member through the StepExecutor registry by the member's step type (not hardcoded to function), proven by a member list containing a non-function member object (no set-membership storage required).

decomposition.intent: Migrate the workflows that route through run_pipeline (run / validation / results endpoints and staging) onto the resolution model, and remove only the execution code this refactor directly supersedes, each removal behavior-preserved by a test.
decomposition.scope: Point the run / validation / results / staging API paths at the unified model; delete superseded execution code. No new scope; behavior preserved across the migration.
acceptance: The run endpoint produces the same response shape and content as before the migration.
The validation and results/staging endpoints produce the same response shape and content as before the migration.
Each piece of superseded execution code removed in this slice is covered by a behavior-preserving test asserting equal endpoint output before and after.
